summary: Reis argues that many data teams are essentially 'LARPing' - going through the motions of doing data work without producing anything that reaches production or delivers business value. He identifies symptoms like endless POCs and complex architectures that look good on paper but never ship, and advises data professionals to either find quick wins to break the cycle or leave for organizations where real work gets done.
key_points: Data teams fall into two categories: those that ship to production and add value, and those stuck in 'Data LARPs' that produce nothing the business uses
POC Purgatory and over-architected solutions are key symptoms of Data LARPing
The AI hype cycle will create even more Data LARPing as companies chase trends without substance
Quick, visible wins for stakeholders are the path out of LARPing - success begets success
If the situation is intractable, the best choice is to leave for an organization doing real work
money_quotes: In Data LARPs, data teams produce very little that the business uses. Often, the data team knows this.
POCs feel like work, but they're just toys.
Especially with the hype around AI, there will be a ton of Data LARPing. Every company wants to get into AI. Very few will get anywhere. Same as it ever was.
If you spend a lot of time working on stuff nobody uses, you're probably LARPing.
key_insight: The greatest danger for data professionals isn't technical failure but organizational theater - going through the motions of 'data work' while never shipping anything that matters.
